title: Correlation Search
description: `correlation_search` contains the data for a correlation search. A correlation search is a recurring search that generates a notable event when search results meet specific conditions. A multi-KPI alert is a type of correlation search.

attributes.name: is_scheduled
attributes.label: is_scheduled
attributes.description: Values: 1 means scheduled; 0 means not scheduled.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.label: disabled
attributes.description: Values: 1 means disabled; 0 means enabled.

attributes.name: cron_schedule
attributes.label: cron_schedule
attributes.description: Schedule searches to run periodically at fixed times, dates, or intervals using a cron expression. Default value is `*/5* * * *` (every 5 minutes).

attributes.name: dispatch.earliest_time
attributes.label: dispatch.earliest_time
attributes.description: Indicates the beginning of the time range for the search. The default value is `-15m`.

attributes.name: dispatch.latest_time
attributes.label: dispatch.latest_time
attributes.description: Indicates the end of the time range for the search. The default value is `-now`.

attributes.name: description
attributes.label: description
attributes.description: A description of the type of issue the search is intended to detect.

attributes.name: search
attributes.label: search
attributes.description: The Splunk search to run.

attributes.name: name
attributes.label: name
attributes.description: A name that describes the correlation search. For example, "cpu_load_percent".

attributes.name: action.itsi_event_generator.param.title
attributes.label: action.itsi_event_generator.param.title
attributes.description: The title to use for the notable event in Episode Review. For example, `mysql-01 server cpu Load %`.

attributes.name: action.itsi_event_generator.param.description
attributes.label: action.itsi_event_generator.param.description
attributes.description: A brief phrase to describe the notable event. For example, "This alert triggers when DB CPU load on the mysql-01 server reaches 80%."

attributes.name: action.itsi_event_generator.param.status
attributes.label: action.itsi_event_generator.param.status
attributes.description: The triage status of the event in Episode Review. You can provide a token in the format `%fieldname%` to substitute the value of a third-party alert field. Values must match an integer specified in `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/SA-ITOA/local/itsi_notable_event_status.conf` or `/default/itsi_notable_event_status.conf` if a local version does not exist. By default, these values are 0-5.

attributes.name: action.itsi_event_generator.param.owner
attributes.label: action.itsi_event_generator.param.owner
attributes.description: The ITSI role to which the notable event is assigned in Episode Review.

attributes.name: action.itsi_event_generator.param.severity
attributes.label: action.itsi_event_generator.param.severity
attributes.description: The level of importance of the event. You can provide a token in the format `%fieldname%` to substitute the value of a third-party alert field. Values must match an integer specified in `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/SA-ITOA/local/itsi_notable_event_severity.conf` or `/default/itsi_notable_event_severity.conf` if a local version does not exist. By default, these values are 1-6.

attributes.name: action.itsi_event_generator.param.drilldown_search_title
attributes.label: action.itsi_event_generator.param.drilldown_search_title
attributes.description: The name of the drilldown search link. You can drill down to a specific Splunk search from an episode in Episode Review.

attributes.name: action.itsi_event_generator.param.drilldown_search_search
attributes.label: action.itsi_event_generator.param.drilldown_search_search
attributes.description: The Splunk search you drill down to.

attributes.name: action.itsi_event_generator.param.drilldown_search_latest_offset
attributes.label: action.itsi_event_generator.param.drilldown_search_latest_offset
attributes.description: Defines how far ahead from the time of the event to look for related events.

attributes.name: action.itsi_event_generator.param.drilldown_search_earliest_offset
attributes.label: action.itsi_event_generator.param.drilldown_search_earliest_offset
attributes.description: Defines how far back from the time of the event to start looking for related events.

attributes.name: action.itsi_event_generator.param.drilldown_title
attributes.label: action.itsi_event_generator.param.drilldown_title
attributes.description: The name of the drilldown website link if you want to drill down to a specific website from the episode in Episode Review.

attributes.name: action.itsi_event_generator.param.drilldown_uri
attributes.label: action.itsi_event_generator.param.drilldown_uri
attributes.description: The website you drill down to.

attributes.name: action.itsi_event_generator.param.event_identifier_fields
attributes.label: action.itsi_event_generator.param.event_identifier_fields
attributes.description: These identifier fields form the event hash field, which is added to every notable event to help identify unique alarm types.

attributes.name: action.itsi_event_generator.param.service_ids
attributes.label: action.itsi_event_generator.param.service_ids
attributes.description: One or more ITSI services to which this correlation search applies. You can only specify services that belong to teams for which you have read access.

attributes.name: action.itsi_event_generator.param.entity_lookup_field
attributes.label: action.itsi_event_generator.param.entity_lookup_field
attributes.description: The field in the data retrieved by the correlation search that is used to look up corresponding entities. For example, `host`.

attributes.name: action.itsi_event_generator.param.search_type
attributes.label: action.itsi_event_generator.param.search_type
attributes.description: search_type = "basic", "composite_kpi_score_type", or composite_kpi_percentage_type

attributes.name: action.itsi_event_generator.param.meta_data
attributes.label: action.itsi_event_generator.param.meta_data
attributes.description: One of two JSON object schemas, depending on whether it is a correlation search or a multi-KPI alert. Correlation search object schema: - threshold_health_score - threshold score set by user - threshold_status - threshold status (default is undefined) - suppression criteria fields - alert_type - score or status - is_suppression - if suppression is enabled or not - is_consecutive - if count is consecutive or not - count - minimum number of times if this alert happens - suppression_period - suppression period in minute if it is non-consecutive - min_alert_period - minimum alert period of selected KPIs - run_every - frequency of search in minutes - score_based_kpis - list of KPIs which is added as part of a composite KPI. Each object in the list must have kpiid - <kpi id>, serviceid - <service id>, urgency - <urgency value> Multi-KPI alert object schema: - time_label - time label for time range - percentage_based_kpis - list of KPIs and service IDs included. Each item should contain kpiid - <kpi id>, serviceid - <service id>, label_thresholds - <threshold and operation type for trigger>. label_thresholds data format is as follows: `{ operation : 'OR', // default for now thresholds : [ { severity: <severity name>, percentage: <percentage value>, percentage_operation: '>=', // default for now } ...... ] }`

attributes.name: action.itsi_event_generator.param.editor
attributes.label: action.itsi_event_generator.param.editor
attributes.description: One of two values: advance_correlation_builder_editor or multi_kpi_alert_editor. It directs to the specific UI page to make edits based on the type of search, correlation search or multi-KPI alert.

attributes.name: action.itsi_event_generator
attributes.label: action.itsi_event_generator
attributes.description: Value: 1

attributes.name: actions
attributes.label: actions
attributes.description: Value: itsi_event_generator

attributes.name: alert.suppress
attributes.label: alert.suppress
attributes.description: Enable suppression to minimize the number of duplicate notable events sent to Episode Review. Values: 1 (means enabled) or 0 (means disabled).

attributes.name: alert.suppress.fields
attributes.label: alert.suppress.fields
attributes.description: The fields to consider when determining if another event matches the current one.

attributes.name: alert.suppress.period
attributes.label: alert.suppress.period
attributes.description: The number of seconds to ignore other events that have the same field values.

attributes.name: action.rss
attributes.label: action.rss
attributes.description: Included in RSS feed. Values: 1 (means enabled) or 0 (means disabled).

attributes.name: action.email
attributes.label: action.email
attributes.description: Send an email when the alert is triggered. Values: 1 (means enabled) or 0 (means disabled).

attributes.name: action.email.to
attributes.label: action.email.to
attributes.description: The email addresses to send the email to.

attributes.name: action.email.subject
attributes.label: action.email.subject
attributes.description: The subject of the email.

attributes.name: action.email.sendcsv
attributes.label: action.email.sendcsv
attributes.description: Send an email in CSV format. Values: 1 (means enabled) or 0 (means disabled).

attributes.name: action.email.sendpdf
attributes.label: action.email.sendpdf
attributes.description: Send an email with a PDF attachment. Values: 1 (means enabled) or 0 (means disabled).

attributes.name: action.email.inline
attributes.label: action.email.inline
attributes.description: Send an email with the text inline. Values: 1 (means enabled) or 0 (means disabled).

attributes.name: action.email.format
attributes.label: action.email.format
attributes.description: Default value is pdf. Other values: html, csv.

attributes.name: action.email.sendresults
attributes.label: action.email.sendresults
attributes.description: Include alert information as an email attachment. Values: 1 (means enabled) or 0 (means disabled).

attributes.name: action.script
attributes.label: action.script
attributes.description: Triggers a shell script if enabled. Values: 1 (means enabled) or 0 (means disabled).

attributes.name: action.script.filename
attributes.label: action.script.filename
attributes.description: Provide the file name of the shell script to run when this alert is triggered.

attributes.name: object_type
attributes.label: object_type
attributes.description: Name of the object type.

attributes.name: create_by
attributes.label: create_by
attributes.description: The user who created this object.

attributes.name: create_source
attributes.label: create_source
attributes.description: The sourcetype initiating create. Has value `manual` for user-initiated creates. For internal use only.

attributes.name: create_time
attributes.label: create_time
attributes.description: Timestamp at the time of creation based on UTC time zone.

attributes.name: mod_source
attributes.label: mod_source
attributes.description: Sourcetype initiating modification. Has value `manual` for user-initiated modifications. For internal use only.

attributes.name: mod_time
attributes.label: mod_time
attributes.description: Timestamp of the last modification based on UTC time zone.

attributes.name: _owner
attributes.label: _owner
attributes.description: Splunk user `nobody`.

attributes.name: _user
attributes.label: _user
attributes.description: User who performed the most recent operation on this object.

attributes.name: version
attributes.label: version
attributes.description: The version of the object. Currently the same as the ITSI app version.
